scenario: Oasis Insurance runs Camunda 8.8 SaaS for 4000 policies per day and is preparing a 60 minutes outage on a non-production environment. The platform lead wants Console to email the on-call engineer whenever workflow errors occur on that environment, and asks which Console capability the introduction page lists for this notification need.
question: Which Camunda Console capability does the introduction page list for getting notified when workflow errors occur on a cluster?

options.text: Configuring Operate incident retention policies on the cluster, after which Operate emits a notification through the orchestration cluster REST API whenever a new incident is created during a workflow run
Subscribing to a Tasklist topic for failed user tasks, after which Tasklist forwards the failure record to a paging service every time a user task on the cluster ends in an error state
Managing alerts in Console, which the introduction page lists as a capability to get notified when workflow errors occur on a cluster managed through the same Console organization
Editing the Zeebe broker logback configuration in Console, after which every workflow error on the cluster is mirrored to an external syslog endpoint configured per environment from Console

a.text: Incorrect. The introduction page does not point at Operate incident retention policies for this notification flow; it lists Manage alerts as the Console capability used to get notified when workflow errors occur.

b.text: Incorrect. There is no Tasklist topic subscription described on the Console introduction page; the listed capability for workflow-error notifications is Manage alerts.

c.text: Correct. The Camunda Console introduction page lists Manage alerts as one of the things you can do using Console, to get notified when workflow errors occur on clusters managed through Console.

d.text: Incorrect. The introduction page does not describe editing Zeebe logback configuration from Console; the alerting capability called out for workflow errors is Manage alerts.

explanation: The Camunda Console introduction lists Manage alerts as a first-class capability for being notified when workflow errors occur. It sits alongside Manage API clients, Manage IP allowlists, and managing the organization, as one of the things an operator can do using Console.
